question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send a single GET /blocks request with no filters, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a full table scan on the record_file table with no WHERE clause, exhausting DB CPU and I/O and causing a non-network DoS for all other users?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the direct string interpolation of filters.orderBy and filters.order into the SQL query in RecordFileService.getBlocks() (e.g., `order by ${filters.orderBy} ${filters.order}`) to inject expensive SQL expressions, causing the DB to perform unbounded computation and resulting in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the direct string interpolation of filters.limit into the SQL query (`limit ${filters.limit}`) in RecordFileService.getBlocks() by manipulating the limit value to an extremely large integer, bypassing the maxLimit check in extractLimitFromFilters() and causing the DB to return and serialize a massive result set, exhausting memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractLimitFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send limit=0 or a negative limit value to the GET /blocks endpoint, and if extractLimitFromFilters() fails to reject it (only checking limit > maxLimit), cause RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a query with LIMIT 0 or an invalid limit, triggering unexpected DB behavior or an unhandled exception that crashes the process?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractLimitFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send limit=NaN or limit=Infinity as a query parameter to GET /blocks, causing extractLimitFromFilters() to pass a non-numeric value into the SQL LIMIT clause via direct interpolation in RecordFileService.getBlocks(), resulting in a DB error or crash that constitutes a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET /blocks/:hashOrNumber request where hashOrNumber is a 96-character hex string (Hedera hash), causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to execute a LIKE query (`HASH like $1` with a 96-char prefix), which performs a full index scan or sequential scan on the record_file table, exhausting DB resources and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validateHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a crafted /:hashOrNumber path parameter that matches the ethHashOrHederaHashPattern regex (`/^(0x)?([0-9A-Fa-f]{64}|[0-9A-Fa-f]{96})$/`) with pathological backtracking input, causing ReDoS in the regex engine and blocking the Node.js event loop, resulting in a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated GET /blocks requests with timestamp=gte:1&timestamp=lte:99999999999999999, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to scan the entire record_file table within that range, exhausting DB CPU and I/O and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: buildAndValidateFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET /blocks request with the maximum allowed number of repeated query parameters (up to config.query.maxRepeatedQueryParameters) for both block.number and timestamp, causing buildAndValidateFilters() and extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to build an extremely large whereQuery array, exhausting CPU during filter processing and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractSqlFromBlockFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET /blocks request with many repeated block.number and timestamp filters at the maximum allowed count, causing extractSqlFromBlockFilters() to generate a WHERE clause with many AND conditions, forcing the DB query planner to evaluate a complex predicate tree and exhausting DB CPU, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=gt:0 and order=asc and limit=100, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to return 100 rows and BlockController.getBlocks() to instantiate 100 BlockViewModel objects each calling addHexPrefix(), nsToSecNs(), and toHexString() on logsBloom (512-byte hex encoding), exhausting CPU in serialization and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: BlockViewModel constructor] Can an unprivileged external user trigger GET /blocks returning rows with large logsBloom values, causing BlockViewModel to call toHexString(recordFile.logsBloom, true, 512) for each row, where the 512-byte hex encoding of many rows in a single response exhausts CPU and memory, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `coalesce(size, length(bytes))` expression in RecordFileService.blocksQuery, where the DB must compute length(bytes) for every row that has a NULL size, causing expensive TOAST decompression of large bytea columns for each row in the result set, exhausting DB I/O and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: generateNextLink()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with a crafted block.number filter that causes generateNextLink() to call utils.getPaginationLink() with a very large index value, and if getPaginationLink() performs expensive URL construction or encoding, exhaust CPU and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send thousands of concurrent GET /blocks/0x<valid-64-char-hex> requests, each causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to execute a LIKE query on the hash column, exhausting the DB connection pool (via config.db.pool.maxConnections) and causing a non-network DoS for all other API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET /blocks/0 request (block number 0), causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to execute `INDEX = $1` with value 0, and if the index column has no entry for 0, trigger a full sequential scan on the record_file table, exhausting DB resources and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: validateHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send a GET /blocks/<very-large-number> where the number is at the boundary of isPositiveLong() (e.g., 2^63-1), causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to pass this value as a BigInt to the DB, and if the DB column is typed as int8, trigger an overflow or type coercion error that crashes the request handler and causes a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with timestamp=gte:0 (minimum timestamp), causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to scan all records from the beginning of time with the default limit, and if the DB lacks an efficient index on consensus_end, exhaust DB I/O and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extractOrderByFromFilters()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with both block.number and timestamp filters, causing extractOrderByFromFilters() to select orderBy based on the first matching filter key, and if the resulting ORDER BY column (index vs consensus_end) lacks an index, force a full table sort in the DB, exhausting memory and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with order=asc and block.number=gt:0, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to execute a forward scan of the entire record_file table ordered by index ascending, and if the table is large, exhaust DB I/O and cause a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: buildWhereSqlStatement()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with the maximum number of repeated timestamp and block.number filters, causing buildWhereSqlStatement() to build a WHERE clause with many AND conditions and a large params array, exhausting memory in the Node.js process during query construction and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getByHashOrNumber()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks/0x<64-char-hex-with-all-zeros> (e.g., 0x0000...0000), causing RecordFileService.getByHashOrNumber() to execute `HASH like '0000...0000%'`, which may match many rows if hashes share a common prefix, forcing the DB to scan many rows and exhausting resources, causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with block.number=gte:0 and block.number=lte:9999999999, causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to generate a WHERE clause with two conditions on the index column covering the entire table, and with the default limit, still force a full index scan, exhausting DB resources and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: extendExpress wrap()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger an unhandled promise rejection in getBlocks() or getByHashOrNumber() by sending a request that causes RecordFileService to throw a non-Error object, causing the wrap() function in extendExpress.js to call next(err) with an unexpected type, crashing the Express error handler and causing a non-network DoS?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/blockRoute.js -> Scope: High: Non-network-based DoS affecting projects with greater than or equal to 25% of the market capitalization on top of the respective layer'] [Function: getBlocks()] Can an unprivileged external user send GET /blocks with a timestamp filter using a very large nanosecond value (e.g., timestamp=gte:99999999999.999999999), causing RecordFileService.getBlocks() to pass this as a string parameter to the DB, and if the DB performs implicit type conversion, trigger an expensive computation or error that causes a non-network DoS?
